def predict_next_frame(frame, action, xy=None):
    """
    Deterministic predictor derived from the observed state changes.
    For each ACTION* we keep only those cell updates that appear in every
    example of that action (i.e., the intersection of all recorded diffs).
    The function applies an update only when the current cell value matches
    the expected old value.
    """
    out = [row[:] for row in frame]

    _diffs = {
        "ACTION1": [
            (63, 12, 6, 0),   # the only change consistently observed
        ],
        "ACTION2": [
            (63, 20, 6, 0), (63, 21, 6, 0),

            (15, 33, 0, 9), (15, 34, 0, 9), (15, 35, 0, 9),
            (15, 38, 9, 2), (15, 39, 9, 0), (15, 40, 9, 0),

            (16, 33, 0, 4), (16, 34, 0, 9), (16, 35, 0, 9),
            (16, 38, 4, 2), (16, 39, 9, 0), (16, 40, 9, 0),

            (17, 33, 0, 9), (17, 34, 0, 9), (17, 35, 0, 9),
            (17, 38, 9, 2), (17, 39, 9, 0), (17, 40, 9, 0),

            (63, 10, 6, 0), (63, 11, 6, 0),
        ],
        "ACTION3": [
            (63, 56, 6, 0),
            (63, 57, 6, 0),
        ],
        "ACTION4": [
            # core pattern at rows 27‑29, cols 22‑24 and 27‑29
            (27, 22, 9, 0), (27, 23, 9, 0), (27, 24, 9, 2),
            (27, 27, 0, 9), (27, 28, 0, 9), (27, 29, 0, 9),

            (28, 22, 9, 0), (28, 23, 9, 0), (28, 24, 4, 2),
            (28, 27, 0, 9), (28, 28, 0, 9), (28, 29, 0, 4),

            (29, 22, 9, 0), (29, 23, 9, 0), (29, 24, 9, 2),
            (29, 27, 0, 9), (29, 28, 0, 9), (29, 29, 0, 9),

            # additional bottom‑row clearing that always appears with ACTION4
            (63, 22, 6, 0),
        ],
    }

    for r, c, old, new in _diffs.get(action, []):
        if 0 <= r < 64 and 0 <= c < 64 and out[r][c] == old:
            out[r][c] = new
    return out

def predict_next_frame(frame, action, xy=None):
    """
    Predicts the next frame by applying the exact cell updates that have been
    observed for each action.  Only those updates that appear in the training
    data are applied; a cell is changed only if its current value matches the
    expected old value.
    """
    out = [row[:] for row in frame]

    _diffs = {
        "ACTION1": [
            (63, 12, 6, 0),   # the sole change consistently seen for ACTION1
        ],
        "ACTION2": [
            (63, 20, 6, 0), (63, 21, 6, 0),

            (15, 33, 0, 9), (15, 34, 0, 9), (15, 35, 0, 9),
            (15, 38, 9, 2), (15, 39, 9, 0), (15, 40, 9, 0),

            (16, 33, 0, 4), (16, 34, 0, 9), (16, 35, 0, 9),
            (16, 38, 4, 2), (16, 39, 9, 0), (16, 40, 9, 0),

            (17, 33, 0, 9), (17, 34, 0, 9), (17, 35, 0, 9),
            (17, 38, 9, 2), (17, 39, 9, 0), (17, 40, 9, 0),

            (63, 10, 6, 0), (63, 11, 6, 0),
        ],
        "ACTION3": [
            (63, 56, 6, 0),
            (63, 57, 6, 0),
        ],
        "ACTION4": [
            # core pattern rows 27‑29 cols 22‑24 and 27‑29
            (27, 22, 9, 0), (27, 23, 9, 0), (27, 24, 9, 2),
            (27, 27, 0, 9), (27, 28, 0, 9), (27, 29, 0, 9),

            (28, 22, 9, 0), (28, 23, 9, 0), (28, 24, 4, 2),
            (28, 27, 0, 9), (28, 28, 0, 9), (28, 29, 0, 4),

            (29, 22, 9, 0), (29, 23, 9, 0), (29, 24, 9, 2),
            (29, 27, 0, 9), (29, 28, 0, 9), (29, 29, 0, 9),

            # bottom‑row clearings that have been observed
            (63, 22, 6, 0),
            (63, 23, 6, 0),
        ],
    }

    for r, c, old, new in _diffs.get(action, []):
        if 0 <= r < 64 and 0 <= c < 64 and out[r][c] == old:
            out[r][c] = new
    return out

def predict_next_frame(frame, action, xy=None):
    """
    Deterministic predictor based on the exact cell updates observed for each
    action.  For every ACTION* we store the set of (row, col, old_val, new_val)
    triples that have appeared in the training data.  When an action is applied,
    a cell is changed only if its current value matches the expected old value.
    """
    out = [row[:] for row in frame]

    _diffs = {
        "ACTION1": [
            (63, 12, 6, 0),   # the sole change consistently seen for ACTION1
        ],
        "ACTION2": [
            (63, 20, 6, 0), (63, 21, 6, 0),

            (15, 33, 0, 9), (15, 34, 0, 9), (15, 35, 0, 9),
            (15, 38, 9, 2), (15, 39, 9, 0), (15, 40, 9, 0),

            (16, 38, 4, 2), (16, 39, 9, 0), (16, 40, 9, 0),

            (17, 33, 0, 9), (17, 34, 0, 9), (17, 35, 0, 9),
            (17, 38, 9, 2), (17, 39, 9, 0), (17, 40, 9, 0),

            (63, 10, 6, 0), (63, 11, 6, 0),
        ],
        "ACTION3": [
            (63, 56, 6, 0),
            (63, 57, 6, 0),
        ],
        "ACTION4": [
            # core pattern rows 27‑29 cols 22‑24 and 27‑29
            (27, 22, 9, 0), (27, 23, 9, 0), (27, 24, 9, 2),
            (27, 27, 0, 9), (27, 28, 0, 9), (27, 29, 0, 9),

            (28, 27, 0, 9), (28, 28, 0, 9), (28, 29, 0, 4),

            # bottom‑row clearings observed with ACTION4

    for r, c, old, new in _diffs.get(action, []):

    return out

def predict_next_frame(frame, action, xy=None):
    """
    Deterministic predictor based on the exact cell updates observed for each
    action.  For every ACTION* we store the set of (row, col, old_val, new_val)
    triples that have appeared in the training data.  When an action is applied,
    a cell is changed only if its current value matches the expected old value.
    """
    out = [row[:] for row in frame]

    _diffs = {
        "ACTION1": [
            (63, 12, 6, 0),
            (63, 27, 6, 0),   # both observed possibilities
        ],
        "ACTION2": [
            (63, 20, 6, 0), (63, 21, 6, 0),

            (15, 33, 0, 9), (15, 34, 0, 9), (15, 35, 0, 9),
            (15, 38, 9, 2), (15, 39, 9, 0), (15, 40, 9, 0),

            (16, 38, 4, 2), (16, 39, 9, 0), (16, 40, 9, 0),

            (17, 33, 0, 9), (17, 34, 0, 9), (17, 35, 0, 9),
            (17, 38, 9, 2), (17, 39, 9, 0), (17, 40, 9, 0),

        ],

            (63, 56, 6, 0),
            (63, 57, 6, 0),
        ],
        "ACTION4": [
            # core pattern rows 27‑29 cols 22‑24 and 27‑29
            (27, 22, 9, 0), (27, 23, 9, 0), (27, 24, 9, 2),
            (27, 27, 0, 9), (27, 28, 0, 9), (27, 29, 0, 9),

            (28, 22, 9, 0), (28, 23, 9, 0), (28, 24, 4, 2),
            (28, 27, 0, 9), (28, 28, 0, 9), (28, 29, 0, 4),

            (29, 22, 9, 0), (29, 23, 9, 0), (29, 24, 9, 2),
            (29, 27, 0, 9), (29, 28, 0, 9), (29, 29, 0, 9),

            # bottom‑row clearings observed with ACTION4
            (63, 22, 6, 0),
            (63, 23, 6, 0),
            (63, 58, 6, 0),
        ],
    }

    for r, c, old, new in _diffs.get(action, []):
        if 0 <= r < 64 and 0 <= c < 64 and out[r][c] == old:
            out[r][c] = new
    return out

def predict_next_frame(frame, action, xy=None):
    """
    Deterministic predictor derived from the observed state changes.
    ACTION4 can produce two distinct groups of updates:
      * a “core” block around rows 27‑29, cols 22‑29,
      * a few isolated bottom‑row clearings (cols 22, 23, 58).
    Which group is applied depends on whether the core block is present in
    the current frame – we test a single representative cell.
    All other actions have a fixed list of updates.
    """
    out = [row[:] for row in frame]

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Fixed diffs for actions that never vary
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    fixed_diffs = {
        "ACTION1": [(63, 12, 6, 0)],                     # only this cell ever changes
        "ACTION2": [
            (63, 20, 6, 0), (63, 21, 6, 0),

            (15, 33, 0, 9), (15, 34, 0, 9), (15, 35, 0, 9),
            (15, 38, 9, 2), (15, 39, 9, 0), (15, 40, 9, 0),

            (16, 33, 0, 4), (16, 34, 0, 9), (16, 35, 0, 9),
            (16, 38, 4, 2), (16, 39, 9, 0), (16, 40, 9, 0),

            (17, 33, 0, 9), (17, 34, 0, 9), (17, 35, 0, 9),
            (17, 38, 9, 2), (17, 39, 9, 0), (17, 40, 9, 0),

            (63, 10, 6, 0), (63, 11, 6, 0),
        ],
        "ACTION3": [(63, 56, 6, 0), (63, 57, 6, 0)],
    }

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # ACTION4 – two possible groups of updates
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    core_block = [
        (27, 22, 9, 0), (27, 23, 9, 0), (27, 24, 9, 2),
        (27, 27, 0, 9), (27, 28, 0, 9), (27, 29, 0, 9),

        (28, 22, 9, 0), (28, 23, 9, 0), (28, 24, 4, 2),
        (28, 27, 0, 9), (28, 28, 0, 9), (28, 29, 0, 4),

        (29, 22, 9, 0), (29, 23, 9, 0), (29, 24, 9, 2),
        (29, 27, 0, 9), (29, 28, 0, 9), (29, 29, 0, 9),
    ]

    bottom_clears = [
        (63, 22, 6, 0),
        (63, 23, 6, 0),
        (63, 58, 6, 0),
    ]

    if action == "ACTION4":
        # decide which group to apply:
        # if the core block is present (the representative cell has its old value)
        if out[27][22] == 9:          # representative of the core pattern
            for r, c, old, new in core_block + bottom_clears:
                if out[r][c] == old:
                    out[r][c] = new
        else:
            for r, c, old, new in bottom_clears:
                if out[r][c] == old:
                    out[r][c] = new
    else:
        for r, c, old, new in fixed_diffs.get(action, []):
            if 0 <= r < 64 and 0 <= c < 64 and out[r][c] == old:
                out[r][c] = new

    return out
